current_session.user_request: Implement 8 professional masterclass features using RLM orchestration

task_queue.description: Prepare and implement Milestone 3 Slice 3 as analyzer-emitted directional EQ repair plus bounded producer mastering mutation

changes.analyzer_directionality: generate_corrections() now emits explicit too-bright vs too-dark adjust_eq detail for centroid-range mismatches only.
changes.producer_directional_eq: ProducerAgent now treats adjust_eq as actionable only when correction detail is explicitly directional, then applies a small clamped brightness/warmth mastering mutation via existing mastering normalization.
changes.retry_routing: Deterministic structured-repair retry now includes directional adjust_eq only when critic metrics are otherwise green, while non-directional adjust_eq remains guidance-only.
changes.proofs: Focused Python and JS regressions cover high/low centroid directionality, conservative mastering mutation, non-directional no-op EQ, and deterministic retry routing.

result.verification: PASS - pytest MUSE/tests/test_output_analyzer.py -q (45 passed) and node copilot-Liku-cli/scripts/test-output-analysis-repair.js (11 passed).

task_queue.description: Implement True Peak Limiter with ISP Detection

output.parametric_eq: RBJ biquad cookbook implementation with Transposed Direct Form II
output.dynamic_eq: Per-band compression with attack/release envelope follower
output.resonance_suppression: FFT peak detection + adaptive notch filters

result.verification: PASS - no errors in Pylance/pyright

changes.producer_directional_eq: ProducerAgent now treats adjust_eq as actionable only when correction detail is explicitly directional, then applies a small clamped brightness/warmth mastering mutation via existing mastering normalization.
changes.retry_routing: Deterministic structured-repair retry now includes directional adjust_eq only when critic metrics are otherwise green, while non-directional adjust_eq remains guidance-only.

Final verifier confirmed the slice stayed bounded to centroid-direction EQ repair only, avoided blind EQ mutation, preserved retry and acceptance/audio gating semantics, and kept non-directional adjust_eq guidance-only.

completed_tasks.description: Complete Milestone 3 Slice 2 by routing actionable structured output-analysis repairs through a deterministic producer retry path

changes.retry_routing: Producer retry selection now prefers a deterministic structured-repair retry when output-analysis corrections are actionable and critic metrics are otherwise green, instead of always broadening immediately into the LLM refinement path.
changes.runtime_safety: The deterministic fast path is intentionally narrow and preserves the existing broader _refineScorePlan() path whenever corrections are missing/non-actionable or critic metrics also failed; acceptance/audio gating semantics remained unchanged.
changes.regressions: Extended the focused JS repair proof script to cover deterministic retry routing, broader-refinement fallback with no corrections, and broader-refinement fallback when critic metrics still fail.
changes.milestone_status: Milestone 3 remains in progress with slices 1-2 complete.

result.verification: PASS - node scripts/test-output-analysis-repair.js passed (8/8). Final verifier confirmed the slice remained bounded to producer/test surfaces, made retry routing more specific in a real way, preserved the broader refinement path when needed, and left acceptance/audio gating semantics intact.

completed_tasks.description: Complete Milestone 3 Slice 1 by translating output-analysis corrections into concrete producer score-plan repairs and reapplying adapted exclusions downstream

changes.producer_repairs: ProducerAgent now translates structured output-analysis corrections into concrete score-plan mutations for mute_drums, swap_instrument -> piano, and adjust_dynamics while keeping adjust_eq conservative guidance-only.
changes.downstream_bridge: score_plan_to_parsed_prompt() now re-applies avoid_instruments and avoid_drums after track-derived adaptation so producer-written repair exclusions become effective downstream instead of remaining schema-valid but inert.
changes.runtime_safety: _passesAudioValidation() and critic-gate bypass semantics stayed unchanged; the slice only deepens repair specificity and downstream leverage.
changes.regressions: Added focused JS producer repair proofs plus Python adapter regressions proving avoided drum/instrument exclusions are re-applied after track adaptation.
changes.milestone_status: Milestone 3 is now in progress with slices 1-2 complete.

result.verification: PASS - node scripts/test-output-analysis-repair.js passed (5/5) and python -m pytest tests/test_output_analyzer.py tests/test_score_plan_adapter.py -q passed (49/49). Final verifier confirmed the slice remained bounded, made translated repair levers effective downstream, and preserved acceptance-gating behavior.

completed_tasks.description: Complete Milestone 2 Slice 2 as explicit-arc-aware primary chord register shaping

changes.orchestration_authority: Added explicit-arc-aware section-wide register lifting on the primary `Chords` track so higher-tension sections can move the main harmonic bed upward by a deterministic octave without changing harmony or density.
changes.runtime_safety: The new register lift applies only when a usable explicit tension arc exists and only when the full shifted section fits the chord instrument range cleanly; missing or unusable arcs remain fail-open at the baseline register.
changes.path_coverage: The register lift is applied on both strategy-returned chords and fallback inline chord generation inside `_create_chord_track()`.
changes.regressions: Added focused tests proving direct register lift, fail-open behavior for missing/unusable arcs, and strategy-path coverage; all targeted adjacent chord/tension regressions remained green.
changes.milestone_status: Milestone 2 remains in progress with slices 1-2 complete.

result.verification: PASS - focused pytest subset passed (59 + 10 + 6 + 7 + 10 individual tests, 92 combined) and post-verifier inspection confirmed the slice remained bounded to primary chord register authority without changing harmony, rhythm, density, motif logic, arranger templates, bass, melody, transitions, or auxiliary layers.

completed_tasks.description: Complete Milestone 2 Slice 1 as tension-controlled auxiliary layer orchestration

changes.orchestration_authority: Added explicit-arc-aware section gating so secondary Brass/Choir layers enter more strongly in moderate/high tension and Texture sections thin or suppress as tension rises.
changes.runtime_safety: New gating applies only when a real usable tension arc exists, preserving fail-open baseline behavior for arrangements with missing or unusable arcs.
changes.regressions: Added focused tests proving auxiliary Brass/Choir activity rises with high tension, Texture activity falls with high tension, and missing arcs still allow baseline auxiliary generation.
changes.milestone_status: Milestone 2 is now in progress with Slice 1 complete.

result.verification: PASS - focused pytest subset passed (7 + 6 + 7 individual tests, 20 combined) and post-verifier inspection confirmed the slice remained bounded to auxiliary-layer orchestration without touching primary chord, bass, melody, motif, or arranger behavior.

completed_tasks.description: Analyze broader genre realism, audio-validation best practices, and instrument-frequency awareness after neo-soul hardening

findings.core_gap: The codebase already contains spectral rules, reference matching, sample sonic fingerprints, and register-aware MIDI validation, but those layers are not consistently wired into rendered-audio scoring, repair loops, or genre-wide coverage.
findings.frequency_awareness: Instrument-aware realism is strongest in MIDI note ranges and sample fingerprints, but there is no unified audio-stage role-band or masking validator connecting instrument identity to expected frequency occupancy.
findings.genre_priorities: After neo_soul, the highest-ROI genres to harden are rnb, trap_soul, house, ambient, and Ethiopian variants because they are parseable/generated but can still fall through output-analysis neutral scoring or inconsistent runtime mappings.
findings.implementation_direction: Best next step is to unify genre normalization and reuse existing OutputAnalyzer, GenreRulesEngine, ReferenceAnalyzer, InstrumentAnalyzer, and StylePolicy modules before inventing new analysis systems.

result.verification: PASS - supervisor plus parallel Explore subagents completed a code-grounded read-only audit and converged on the same architecture gaps and rollout priorities.

completed_tasks.description: Gate producer success on rendered-audio validation and normalize neo-soul runtime support

changes.producer.js: Producer success now requires rendered-audio validation when audio exists; critic-gate bypass no longer accepts audio-validation failures.
changes.output_analyzer.py: Added explicit neo_soul and rnb_neosoul output-analysis targets plus onset-density scoring for rhythm-fit penalties.
changes.runtime_normalization: Prompt parsing, microtiming presets, and performance-model mappings now treat neo_soul as a first-class genre instead of falling back through generic rnb handling.

result.verification: PASS - focused pytest suite passed (38 output-analyzer tests, 3 prompt-parser tests) and a stubbed ProducerAgent smoke check proved critics-pass plus audio-fail now returns COMPLETED_WITH_CRITIC_FAIL while audio-pass returns COMPLETED_SUCCESS.

completed_tasks.description: Investigate listened neo-soul quality complaints against generation and validation code paths

findings.validation_gap: ProducerAgent accepts success on MIDI critics alone; rendered-audio analysis is recorded but does not gate success.
findings.genre_gap: OutputAnalyzer has no neo_soul target profile, so GenreMatchScorer returns the neutral fallback score 0.75 for neo_soul outputs.
findings.rhythm_gap: Rendered-audio analysis uses spectral/percussive heuristics only and does not measure groove pocket, beat stability, or microtiming feel.
findings.generation_gap: Performance-model and microtiming mappings are stronger for rnb than neo_soul, so neo_soul can fall back to generic role defaults in some timing paths.

result.verification: PASS - code inspection plus render reports showed neo_soul render reports repeatedly report genre_match_score=0.75 with passed=true, matching the unknown-genre fallback in output_analyzer.py.
